questions.question: An [[n, k, d]] stabilizer code has how many independent stabilizer generators?

options.text: n generators
k generators
n - k generators
n + k generators

questions.explanation: A stabilizer code with n physical qubits encoding k logical qubits has n-k independent generators. This leaves a 2ᵏ-dimensional code space as the +1 eigenspace.

questions.question: What is the normalizer N(S) of a stabilizer group S?

options.text: The stabilizer group itself
All Paulis that commute with every element of S
All Paulis that anticommute with S
The center of the Pauli group

questions.explanation: The normalizer N(S) consists of all Pauli operators that commute with every element of S. It contains S as a subgroup, and the logical operators are in N(S)\S (normalizer minus stabilizer).

questions.question: For the [[5,1,3]] code, what is the dimension of the code space?
questions.explanation: The code space dimension is 2ᵏ where k is the number of logical qubits. For [[5,1,3]], k=1, so dim = 2¹ = 2. This encodes one logical qubit (|0̄⟩ and |1̄⟩).

questions.question: What does the code distance d represent?

options.text: Number of physical qubits
Minimum weight of any stabilizer
Minimum weight of any non-trivial logical operator
Maximum number of correctable errors

questions.explanation: The distance d is the minimum weight of any Pauli in N(S)\S, i.e., the lightest non-trivial logical operator. A code can correct ⌊(d-1)/2⌋ errors.

questions.question: When is a single Pauli error E correctable by a stabilizer code?

options.text: When E commutes with all stabilizers
When E is a stabilizer or anticommutes with some stabilizer
When E has weight less than d
When E is a logical operator

questions.explanation: Error E is correctable if either: (1) E is a stabilizer (does nothing to code space), or (2) E anticommutes with some stabilizer (detected via syndrome). Errors in N(S)\S that commute with all stabilizers are logical errors.

questions.question: What must logical operators satisfy?

options.text: Commute with all stabilizers and be stabilizers themselves
Anticommute with all stabilizers
Commute with all stabilizers but not be stabilizers
Have weight equal to the distance

questions.explanation: Logical operators must commute with all stabilizers (to preserve the code space) but not be stabilizers themselves (to act non-trivially on encoded information). They live in N(S)\S.

questions.question: A code with distance d=5 can correct any 2 Pauli errors.
questions.explanation: True. A code corrects ⌊(d-1)/2⌋ errors. For d=5, this is ⌊4/2⌋ = 2 errors. This works because any product of up to 4 Paulis either has unique syndrome or is a stabilizer.
